COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND IDENTIFIES THE FULL TITLE OF THE DOCUMENTS TO BE SEALED AS SHOWN IN THE REGISTER OF ACTIONS, INCLUDING THE TRANSACTION ID NUMBER AND THE DATE THE DOCUMENT WAS FILED. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-22-2023 Continued To Oct-18-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-09-2023 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BLOOMJOY INC., DAVID PHILLIPS' DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):

The matter was discussed and argued. After hearing, the court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS BLOOMJOY INC., DAVID PHILLIPS DEMURRER to COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendants' demurrer to the complaint is overruled.

The demurrer is based largely on defendants' spin of five extrinsic documents neither attached to nor referenced in the complaint. Defendants' request for judicial notice of those extrinsic documents is denied. The documents' contents are not the kind of "undisputable" "facts and propositions" of which judicial notice is taken. (Evid. Code 452(h); see also Gould v. Maryland Sound Industries, Inc. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 1137, 1144-45.)

Relying on dicta in footnotes, defendants argue that judicial notice may be taken of the "contents of documents referenced in a complaint." (RJN 2:19-27, 3:20-23.) That notion is dubious but irrelevant here in any event, as the five documents are not referenced in the complaint. Rather, it is alleged that "an agreement" was "partially written, partially oral and partially implied by conduct." (Cmplt. 9:1-2.)
Denial of judicial notice eviscerates the demurrer. Rather than parse defendants' arguments to see what, if anything, remains, the court grants leave to file another demurrer - after compliance with CCP 430.41.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)
